title: Dog Stiff Neck and Screaming: Causes & Emergency Steps
meta_desc: Is your dog screaming when touched and holding their neck stiffly? Learn why this is an emergency and how to help your pet immediately.
content_html:
What is causing your dog's neck pain and screaming?

A dog that presents with a stiff neck and vocalizes in pain when touched is likely experiencing a severe neurological or musculoskeletal issue. Common causes include Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD), which involves a herniated disc in the cervical spine, or Meningitis-Polyarteritis. These conditions cause intense nerve compression or inflammation, leading to sharp pain even with light contact or subtle movement.

Is this a veterinary emergency?

Yes, this is a high-urgency medical emergency. Intense pain combined with a stiff neck can indicate spinal cord compression that may lead to permanent paralysis if not treated immediately. If your dog is also stumbling, unable to walk, or acting lethargic, you should seek emergency veterinary care right away. Do not attempt to massage the neck or give human pain medications, as these can cause fatal side effects or complicate treatment.

How a photo or video helps triage

While a photo can show your dog's rigid posture, a short video of your dog's movement is even more helpful for triage. Recording how they carry their head or any 'knuckling' (walking on the tops of their paws) allows a veterinarian to assess the level of neurological deficit. This documentation helps the clinic prepare for your arrival and determines if you need to be seen ahead of other patients.

faqs.question: Can I give my dog aspirin for neck pain?
faqs.answer: No, never give human medications like aspirin or ibuprofen. These can cause fatal stomach ulcers or organ failure and prevent your vet from using safer, more effective prescription anti-inflammatories.

faqs.question: How should I transport my dog to the vet?
faqs.answer: Keep your dog as still as possible on a flat, firm surface or in a small carrier. Avoid using a neck collar and do not allow them to jump or climb stairs during transport.

faqs.question: What if my dog is also shaking or trembling?
faqs.answer: Trembling is a common sign of severe pain or shock. This reinforces the need for immediate emergency care, as it suggests the dog's body is struggling to cope with the intensity of the injury.
